rubric_items.reasoning: Agent queried both SHOW CLUSTER SETTING version (24.1) and SELECT version() (CockroachDB CCL v24.1.0), confirmed they matched, and checked preserve_downgrade_option was unset—correctly concluding the cluster is fully upgraded to 24.1 with no pending finalization. It then created the ConfigMap with db_version=24.1 and verified it via get -o yaml. This is a straightforward read-and-record task with no complex state to break, so the fix is durable and would survive a restart or re-check.

Clean, logical sequence: gathered all three diagnostic facts (cluster setting, binary version, downgrade option) in one batched command, discovered the ConfigMap didn't exist yet, created it, then verified the result. No backtracking, no wasted actions, no trial-and-error.

Used only read-only kubectl exec/get calls for diagnosis and a single targeted `kubectl create configmap --from-literal` for the one required mutation. No broad or risky commands, all scoped to the cockroachdb namespace and directly relevant to the task.
Exactly one mutation (the ConfigMap creation) containing exactly the required key, with no extraneous changes or collateral churn. Matches the API activity log (12 calls total, 1 mutation).
